summary: Backend-only testing of the Stripe Payments & Financial Management module. Built /app/backend/tests/test_payments_finance.py (26 pytest cases) covering /payments/config, hosted Checkout smoke (Stripe URL returned, payment_status=pending), TEST-ONLY authorize-test endpoint (real Stripe test calls with pm_card_visa -> payment_status=authorized, ~20% commission split), GET /payments/orders/{id}/status reconciliation, admin manual capture (captures funds & writes a 'capture' entry to payment_transactions ledger), auto-capture on driver-driven delivery (accept -> authorize -> advance x N -> delivered -> payment_status=captured), cancel-authorization (releases hold -> canceled), driver wallet shape & values, cash-out validation (amount<10 -> 400, amount>balance -> 400, valid -> pending), withdrawal admin approve/pay (with reference)/reject (with reason) transitions, admin financials overview KPI shape + 14-day series, transactions pagination, /admin/payments/authorized listing, and all auth guards (admin-only for capture/cancel/financials, driver-only for /wallet/*, owner/admin for /payments/orders/{id}/status). 26/26 PASS against the public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL with the live Stripe TEST secret key.

minor.issue: Admin can approve/pay a withdrawal without checking that the driver still has sufficient available_balance at approval time (no re-validation). available_balance is, however, computed correctly off the withdrawal_requests collection, so funds are accounted for once a request exists.

When utype=='driver', the guard allows access if order.driver_id is None (unassigned). In practice any logged-in driver could read the payment status of any unassigned order. Low impact (no sensitive PII in the response) but worth tightening.

action_items: (Optional) Re-validate available_balance inside _process_withdrawal before transitioning to 'approved'/'paid' to guard against race conditions or admin errors.
(Optional) Tighten driver guard on GET /payments/orders/{id}/status so unassigned drivers cannot read other shippers' orders.
critical_code_review_comments: services/payments.py + server.py correctly use STRIPE_API_KEY from backend/.env (with STRIPE_SECRET_KEY fallback) and gate authorize-test on is_test_key() so it cannot run with a live key.
_apply_intent_to_order recomputes the commission split using getattr(intent, 'amount_received') for captures, which correctly handles partial captures.
advance_order's auto-capture branch is invoked when status transitions to 'delivered' and the test confirmed it works end-to-end against the real Stripe API.
All payment_transactions / orders / withdrawal_requests queries already exclude MongoDB '_id'.

server.py is ~5750 lines -- continues to exceed the 700-line guideline. Strongly recommend extracting routers into modules (payments_router.py, wallet_router.py, admin_financials_router.py) in a follow-up.
Admin notifications are emitted on every withdrawal transition (approve/pay/reject) -- verified via the admin withdrawal listing.
seed_data_creation: Used POST /api/seed-demo. Created several TEST_ shipments (cargo_description=TEST_Boxes_*) which exercised authorize/capture/cancel flows and a handful of small (€10/€11/€12) withdrawal requests against the demo driver. All authorize calls used the Stripe test card pm_card_visa; captures were performed via the real Stripe test API.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_payments_finance.py as the regression suite for the payments module. The suite assumes EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is reachable and that STRIPE_API_KEY (sk_test_*) is configured server-side. All tests are idempotent given the seed-demo bootstrap. Note that the backend emits a non-blocking 'EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY missing or invalid' warning during order status transitions -- this is unrelated to payments and does not affect any payment behaviour.
